connections.def: from 3523; to abstain from food (religiously):--fast. see GREEK for
from the perfect tense of 2476; to be stationary, i.e. (figuratively) to persevere:--stand (fast). see GREEK for

connections.gloss: hold (by, fast), keep, lay hand (hold) on, obtain, retain, take (by)
connections.def: from 2904; to use strength, i.e. seize or retain (literally or figuratively):--hold (by, fast), keep, lay hand (hold) on, obtain, retain, take (by). see GREEK for

connections.gloss: abide fast, cleave (fast together), follow close (hard after), be joined (together), keep (fast), overtake, pursue hard, stick, take.

aid, amend, [idiom] calker, catch, cleave, confirm, be constant, constrain, continue, be of good (take) courage(-ous, -ly), encourage (self), be established, fasten, force, fortify, make hard, harden, help, (lay) hold (fast), lean, maintain, play the man, mend, become (wax) mighty, prevail, be recovered, repair, retain, seize, be (wax) sore, strengthen (self), be stout, be (make, shew, wax) strong(-er), be sure, take (hold), be urgent, behave self valiantly, withstand.
have, hold (fast), keep (in memory), let, X make toward, possess, retain, seize on, stay, take, withhold
connections.def: from 2596 and 2192; to hold down (fast), in various applications (literally or figuratively):--have, hold (fast), keep (in memory), let, X make toward, possess, retain, seize on, stay, take,…

connections.gloss: bind, fast, gird, harness, hold, keep, make ready, order, prepare, prison(-er), put in bonds, set in array, tie.
(be fast a-, be in a deep, cast into a dead, that) sleep(-er, -eth).
